name: Valley Isle Community
description_short: Valley Isle Community Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Maui residents and workers with checking, savings, lending, and credit card products.
description_long:
Valley Isle Community Federal Credit Union (VIC FCU) is a federally-chartered, not-for-profit credit union serving the Maui community. The institution operates under NCUA insurance and membership is open to anyone who lives or works on Maui. The credit union has been established in the community with multiple branch locations across the island, including offices in Kahului, Wailuku, and Lahaina.

VIC FCU offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial services. Personal products include checking and savings accounts, share certificates, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards (Visa Platinum), mortgage loans, and home equity lines of credit. Business members can access business checking, business savings, and business loan products. The credit union also provides convenient digital services including online banking, mobile banking, telephone banking, and ATM access through the nationwide Co-op Network, with access to over 5,000 shared branches nationwide and 60+ branches statewide in Hawaii.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its community focus and member accessibility. All three physical branches are located on Maui with consistent weekday hours (8:30 am–4:30 pm), and membership eligibility is straightforward based on geographic residence or employment. VIC FCU participates in shared branching and the Co-op Network ATM system, providing members access far beyond their local branches. The institution also offers competitive rates, with share certificates at 3.50% APY and new auto loans starting at 4.75% APR.

As a credit union, VIC FCU prioritizes member benefits over profit maximization. However, the institution serves a geographically limited membership base (Maui only), which may restrict growth and product innovation compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Members benefit from the not-for-profit structure and community-focused mission, though product offerings and technology may be more limited than larger financial institutions.
services: Auto loans (4.75% APR for new vehicles)
Co-op Network shared branch access (5,000+ nationwide)
pros: Simple membership eligibility—anyone who lives or works on Maui can join
Three physical branch locations across Maui with consistent business hours
Access to 5,000+ shared branches nationwide and 60+ in Hawaii through Co-op Network
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.75% APR for new vehicles
Share certificates offering 3.50% APY, competitive for credit unions
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, mortgages, HELOCs, and business accounts
Digital banking options including mobile app, online banking, and telephone banking
cons: Limited geographic footprint—membership restricted to Maui residents and workers only
Modest savings rates on checking (0.01% APY) and savings (0.05% APY) accounts
Personal loan APR starting at 7.25% is standard but not exceptionally competitive
Limited branch hours with mid-day lunch closures at two locations
Smaller institution may have fewer technology features and product innovations compared to larger banks
best_for: Maui residents and workers seeking community-focused financial services with local branch access
Members wanting to support a not-for-profit, member-owned institution
Small business owners on Maui needing business checking and loan products
Consumers who value nationwide branch and ATM access through shared branching networks
diagnosis: Valley Isle Community FCU is best suited for Maui-based consumers who prioritize local branch relationships, community values, and member-focused service over maximum rate competitiveness. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Maui membership, making it unavailable to consumers outside the island.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Valley Isle Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Kahului, HI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
